category_title: Collaborative students performing as rhythmic poetry readers
minimal_context: Bill and Kwame are sharing a poem with the class at the end of a reading workshop.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Bill and Kwame, it is time for the whole-class share session. Please read your poem together.

STUDENT: (Tapping pencils on desks and moving their bodies from side to side) This is the way / Hey! / we start the day / Hey!

TUTOR: Read the next part.

STUDENT: (Taking turns to read the poem in singing voices) we get the knowledge / Hey! / to go to college / Hey!

TUTOR: Finish the last few lines.

STUDENT: (Swinging the poetry packet over his head and continuing in a singing voice) we won ’t stop there / Hey! / go anywhere / Hey!

options.A: Inviting the students to demonstrate collaborative reading and framing their action as 'noticing the rhythm.'
options.B: Following the students' lead and maintaining a physical distance during the whole-class share session.
options.C: Teaching the students the technique of 'stepping back' and not acting on their 'first thought.'
options.D: Making sure that their presentation of the poem is accurate and meets the activity objectives.
